findings: Found 1 fresh fixture/snapshot stale-protection issue: the CLI i18n install snapshot pins stale Cursor hook/skill capability output despite current installer/parity evidence that Cursor hooks/bootstrap are delivered.
issues_found: [{"title":"CLI i18n snapshot pins stale Cursor hook/skill capability output","severity":"medium","description":"The install i18n snapshot locks the Cursor row as `hook=n/a`, `skill=n/a`, and `manual step required`, while the current installer writes Cursor hook config/bootstrap and the parity matrix explicitly says Cursor has full skill/hook/mcp support. The snapshot test only accepts the full sanitized stdout, so updating it with `vitest -u` can continue protecting the stale capability presentation instead of asserting the semantic contract that installed Cursor assets must be reflected in the capability summary. This is distinct from the registered Cursor docs drift: it is a test/fixture baseline that preserves an incorrect runtime capability display.","location":"packages/cli/__tests__/__snapshots__/i18n.test.ts.snap:47","fix_direction":"Replace the broad stdout snapshot for the capability table with semantic assertions over parsed rows, and fix `detectClientSupports` so Cursor reports hook/bootstrap assets after install; keep the snapshot only for locale copy once the row semantics are asserted.","affected_components":["packages/cli/__tests__/__snapshots__/i18n.test.ts.snap","packages/cli/__tests__/i18n.test.ts","packages/cli/src/config/resolver.ts","packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts","packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts","packages/shared/src/schemas/parity-matrix.ts"],"perspective":"fixture-snapshot-stale-protection"}]
